refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity output of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with system capability. Refinement separates the functional requirement (enable task execution at target productivity) from the verification criterion (measured output ≥ target). This clarifies that the system must provide the operational capability; measurement and reporting are separate concerns. High safety criticality requires explicit safety constraint linkage.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period; measure actual m³/h or ton/h output; cross-check against worksite safety compliance logs and operational policy adherence. Verify that productivity is achievable without safety or policy violations.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The NGHE solution shall provide task execution capability (motion, control, sensing) sufficient to achieve target productivity rates.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The NGHE solution shall measure and report productivity metrics (m³/h or ton/h) in real-time or post-operation.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall enforce worksite safety constraints and operational policy compliance during task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall support both on-site and remote (tele-operated) task execution modes.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate high-performance vehicle platform with real-time teleoperation capability, instrumented with productivity sensors (load cells, GPS, inertial measurement), and enforce safety interlocks via Safety Services module.
DP-B: Deploy a modular vehicle with plug-and-play task-specific attachments, coupled with adaptive teleoperation and continuous safety monitoring via distributed safety agents.

DP-C: Implement a centralized task planning and execution engine that coordinates vehicle motion, attachment control, and real-time safety validation against worksite policies, with teleoperation as a fallback mode.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Integrate high-performance vehicle platform with real-time teleoperation capability, instrumented with productivity sensors (load cells, GPS, inertial measurement), and enforce safety interlocks via Safety Services module. This approach directly maps task execution capability (vehicle + teleoperation) to productivity measurement (sensors) and safety compliance (Safety Services interlocks), minimizing architectural diffusion and maintaining clear ownership boundaries.

potential_sources: Vehicle motion control and Teleoperation command latency coupling: mitigated by real-time network link and deterministic control loops.
Productivity sensor calibration and Safety Services validation coupling: mitigated by explicit sensor-to-safety data flow via Telemetry interface.
Worksite policy enforcement and vehicle capability mismatch: mitigated by pre-deployment policy validation and runtime safety interlocks.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling because each functional concern (task execution, measurement, safety) maps to a distinct module (Vehicle, instrumentation, Safety Services) with well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert). No circular dependencies detected.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle platform has sufficient actuation bandwidth and control authority to achieve target productivity rates. Risk: If vehicle is under-powered or slow-responding, productivity target may be unachievable regardless of teleoperation quality.
Assumption: Productivity sensors (load cells, GPS) are calibrated and reliable. Risk: Sensor drift or failure could lead to incorrect productivity reporting and false acceptance/rejection.

Assumption: Safety Services module can enforce worksite policies in real-time without introducing unacceptable latency. Risk: Safety interlocks may slow task execution, reducing effective productivity.

Assumption: Teleoperation network link provides sufficient bandwidth and latency for real-time control. Risk: Network congestion or latency spikes could degrade operator control and reduce productivity.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-A to explicitly specify: (1) vehicle actuation performance targets (response time, force/torque capacity); (2) sensor accuracy and calibration requirements; (3) Safety Services latency budget (max acceptable delay for safety interlocks); (4) network link SLA (bandwidth, latency, availability). These specifications will enable rigorous DP-IM mapping and risk mitigation.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system integration boundary that orchestrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to deliver productivity-enabled task execution. It owns the end-to-end functional flow: operator command → teleoperation → vehicle motion → productivity measurement → safety validation → task completion.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h, task progress), vehicle state (position, orientation, load), and sensor readings (GPS, inertial, load cells) to external monitoring systems (Site Manager dashboard, Maintenance systems).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives task definitions (target location, attachment type, worksite constraints) from Site Manager or autonomous task planner. Tasking includes worksite policy parameters and safety zone definitions.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits safety alerts (policy violations, sensor faults, emergency stops) to Safety Manager and on-site personnel. Alerts trigger corrective actions or task suspension.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link for bidirectional communication with Tele-operator (command/feedback) and remote monitoring systems. Network Link carries teleoperation commands, vehicle telemetry, and safety alerts.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level integration boundary. It owns the end-to-end productivity delivery flow and coordinates all sub-systems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Minimal diffusion: only external stakeholder interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary. High containment because all internal functional logic (task execution, measurement, safety enforcement) is encapsulated within NGHE Solution and its sub-modules.

Vehicle module owns motion control and actuation but does not own teleoperation logic, productivity measurement, or safety policy enforcement. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Vehicle alone would require Vehicle to interface with external Teleoperation and Safety Services, creating high diffusion. Vehicle is a sub-component of NGHE Solution, not the top-level boundary.

Teleoperation module owns remote command translation and feedback but does not own vehicle actuation, productivity measurement, or safety enforcement. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Teleoperation alone would require it to coordinate with Vehicle and Safety Services, creating high diffusion and unclear ownership of end-to-end productivity delivery.

Safety Services module owns policy validation and safety interlocks but does not own task execution, teleoperation, or productivity measurement. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Safety Services alone would create high diffusion because Safety Services is a cross-cutting concern, not the primary owner of productivity delivery.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary because it owns the end-to-end functional flow from task input (Tasking) through execution (Vehicle + Teleoperation) to measurement (Telemetry) and safety validation (SafetyAlert). All sub-functions (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) are contained within NGHE Solution's scope, with external interfaces limited to stakeholder communication (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link). This minimizes diffusion and creates a clear, testable system boundary.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may become a 'god module' if sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are not properly decomposed and owned. Mitigation: Ensure each sub-module has clear, atomic responsibilities and well-defined interfaces.

Risk: Productivity measurement (FR-NGHE-001.2) may be diffused across Vehicle (sensor hardware), Teleoperation (data transmission), and external monitoring systems (data aggregation). Mitigation: Assign explicit ownership of productivity calculation and reporting to a sub-module (e.g., Telemetry aggregator within NGHE Solution).

Risk: Safety policy enforcement (FR-NGHE-001.3) may conflict with productivity optimization (FR-NGHE-001.1), creating latency or capability trade-offs. Mitigation: Establish clear safety-productivity trade-off criteria and validate via gate review.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Decompose NGHE Solution into explicit sub-modules at the next level: (1) Vehicle Control Module (owns motion and actuation); (2) Teleoperation Module (owns remote command translation); (3) Productivity Measurement Module (owns sensor aggregation and metric calculation); (4) Safety Enforcement Module (owns policy validation and interlocks). Each sub-module should have clear ownership, atomic responsibilities, and well-defined interfaces. This will reduce diffusion and enable rigorous DP-IM mapping at the next decomposition level.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (integrated vehicle + teleoperation + safety services) directly addresses the refined FR (enable task execution at target productivity while maintaining safety compliance). The DP specifies a coherent solution approach: vehicle provides motion capability, teleoperation enables remote control, sensors measure productivity, and Safety Services enforces policy constraints. No logical gaps or contradictions detected. DP-A is implementable and testable.

diffusion_risks: Teleoperation command latency may diffuse across Network Link (transport), Teleoperation module (command translation), and Vehicle (control loop). Mitigation: Establish end-to-end latency budget and allocate to each component.

Productivity measurement may diffuse across Vehicle (sensor hardware), Telemetry interface (data transmission), and external monitoring systems (aggregation). Mitigation: Assign explicit ownership of metric calculation to NGHE Solution sub-module.

Safety policy enforcement may diffuse across Safety Services (validation logic), Vehicle (motion constraints), and Teleoperation (command filtering). Mitigation: Establish clear safety enforcement hierarchy and interface contracts.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary because it owns the end-to-end functional flow and coordinates all sub-systems. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-modules within NGHE Solution, not peer modules. This containment structure minimizes diffusion and creates clear ownership boundaries. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and limited to stakeholder communication.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: FR-NGHE-001 can be verified via representative operations trial: measure actual m³/h or ton/h output, cross-check against worksite safety compliance logs, and validate that productivity ≥ target without policy violations. Verification is feasible because NGHE Solution owns the end-to-end flow and can instrument all critical points (task input, vehicle motion, sensor output, safety validation, task completion).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-NGHE-001 is allocated to NGHE Solution with clear sub-allocations: FR-NGHE-001.1 (task execution) → Vehicle + Teleoperation; FR-NGHE-001.2 (productivity measurement) → Telemetry + instrumentation; FR-NGHE-001.3 (safety compliance) → Safety Services; FR-NGHE-001.4 (dual-mode operation) → Teleoperation + Vehicle. Each sub-FR is traceable to a specific module or interface, enabling rigorous verification and traceability.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-A to specify vehicle actuation performance targets (response time, force/torque capacity), sensor accuracy requirements, Safety Services latency budget, and network link SLA. These specifications will enable rigorous DP-IM mapping and risk mitigation at the next level.

Decompose NGHE Solution into explicit sub-modules (Vehicle Control, Teleoperation, Productivity Measurement, Safety Enforcement) with clear ownership, atomic responsibilities, and well-defined interfaces. This will reduce diffusion and enable rigorous functional decomposition at the next level.
Establish explicit safety-productivity trade-off criteria and validate via gate review. Define acceptable latency/capability trade-offs between safety interlocks and productivity optimization.

Define network link SLA (bandwidth, latency, availability) and validate that teleoperation can achieve target productivity within these constraints. Conduct latency sensitivity analysis to identify critical paths.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that requires decomposition into atomic sub-FRs (task execution, measurement, safety compliance, dual-mode operation). DP-A is a coherent solution approach but requires refinement to specify performance targets and latency budgets. NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary, but requires decomposition into sub-modules with clear ownership and interfaces. The next level of functional decomposition will refine each sub-FR (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) into atomic requirements, select DPs for each, and assign to sub-modules (Vehicle Control, Teleoperation, Productivity Measurement, Safety Enforcement). This hierarchical decomposition will enable rigorous verification and risk mitigation.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The NGHE solution shall provide task execution capability (motion, control, sensing) sufficient to achieve target productivity rates.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The NGHE solution shall measure and report productivity metrics (m³/h or ton/h) in real-time or post-operation.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall enforce worksite safety constraints and operational policy compliance during task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall support both on-site and remote (tele-operated) task execution modes.

assumptions: Vehicle platform has sufficient actuation bandwidth and control authority to achieve target productivity rates.
Productivity sensors (load cells, GPS, inertial measurement) are calibrated, reliable, and provide sufficient accuracy for productivity measurement.
Safety Services module can enforce worksite policies in real-time without introducing unacceptable latency that degrades productivity.
Teleoperation network link provides sufficient bandwidth and latency for real-time control and meets agreed SLA.
Worksite safety plans and operational policies are well-defined, measurable, and enforceable via automated interlocks.
Operator (on-site or remote) has sufficient training and situational awareness to execute tasks safely and productively.
open_risks: Vehicle under-powered or slow-responding: productivity target may be unachievable regardless of teleoperation quality. Mitigation: Conduct vehicle performance validation against target productivity rates.
Sensor drift or failure: incorrect productivity reporting and false acceptance/rejection. Mitigation: Implement sensor health monitoring and calibration procedures.
Safety interlocks introduce unacceptable latency: effective productivity reduced below target. Mitigation: Establish latency budget and conduct sensitivity analysis.
Network congestion or latency spikes: operator control degraded, productivity reduced. Mitigation: Define network link SLA and conduct latency stress testing.
Safety-productivity trade-off conflicts: unclear which takes priority in edge cases. Mitigation: Establish explicit trade-off criteria and validate via gate review.
Worksite policy enforcement diffused across multiple modules: unclear ownership and potential gaps. Mitigation: Assign explicit ownership to Safety Services sub-module and define interface contracts.
evidence_needed: Vehicle performance specification: actuation response time, force/torque capacity, control bandwidth.
Sensor accuracy and calibration specification: load cell accuracy, GPS precision, inertial measurement unit drift rates.
Safety Services latency budget: maximum acceptable delay for policy validation and safety interlocks.
Network link SLA: bandwidth, latency, availability requirements for teleoperation.
Worksite safety plan and operational policy definitions: measurable constraints and enforcement mechanisms.
Representative operations trial results: measured productivity (m³/h or ton/h), safety compliance logs, operator feedback.
Latency sensitivity analysis: impact of network latency on operator control and productivity.
Safety-productivity trade-off criteria: acceptable latency/capability trade-offs between safety and productivity.
